items.title: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Just Became Too Cheap to Ignore
items.summary: Microsoft stock has declined 13% year-to-date and trades at a P/E ratio of 25, presenting a buying opportunity despite strong fundamentals. The company reported 18% revenue growth and 23% net income growth in Q3 FY2026, driven primarily by Microsoft Cloud and a booming AI business that grew 123% year-over-year with a $37 billion annual revenue run rate. The company's agentic AI agents are gaining traction with enterprise clients, positioning Microsoft to potentially accelerate revenue growth to 20%+ in future quarters.

items.title: Nvidia's Jensen Huang Just Delivered Big News to Shareholders. And It May Influence Your Decision on Whether to Buy Nvidia Stock Right Now.
items.summary: During Nvidia's earnings call, CEO Jensen Huang announced that agentic AI has arrived, shifting demand from just training large language models to ongoing compute needs for AI agents to perform real-world tasks. This addresses investor concerns about the sustainability of infrastructure spending. With Nvidia's Blackwell platform gaining traction and the new Vera Rubin platform launching in Q3, the company is positioned for continued growth. Nvidia's valuation has become more attractive at 25x forward earnings, down from 40x at the start of the year, making the stock appear to be a buy.

items.title: The AI Stock I'm Buying for My Retirement Portfolio -- and Why It Has Nothing to Do With Hype
items.summary: Alphabet is highlighted as a top AI stock for long-term retirement investing due to its full-stack AI capabilities, strong revenue growth across multiple business segments (Google Search +19% YoY, Google Cloud +63% YoY), and competitive positioning. Despite massive capex spending of $180-190 billion in 2026, the company's valuation remains reasonable at 27x forward earnings with a PEG ratio below 0.7.

items.title: 3 Monster Stocks to Hold for the Next 20 Years
items.summary: The article identifies Amazon, O'Reilly Automotive, and Nike as 'monster stocks' with strong long-term compounding potential over the next 20 years. Amazon benefits from multiple high-margin growth engines including AWS, advertising, and custom silicon. O'Reilly Automotive is supported by an aging vehicle fleet and the shift toward independent repair shops. Nike maintains global brand dominance despite current margin pressures. Key risks include regulatory action for Amazon, EV transition for O'Reilly, and competition for Nike.

items.title: Planet Fitness Grew Revenue 22%, So Why Did One Investor Trim $20 Million?
items.summary: Dorsal Capital Management sold $19.78 million worth of Planet Fitness shares in Q1 2026, reducing its stake to 7.3% of assets under management. Despite the company's strong 22% revenue growth and 20% EBITDA increase, the stock has declined 50% over the past year due to slower-than-expected member growth in 2026, prompting management to pause a planned price increase and intensify marketing efforts.

items.title: 3 Top AI Stocks to Buy With $1,000 Right Now
items.summary: The article recommends three AI stocks for investors with $1,000: Amazon (benefiting from AWS cloud computing and custom AI chips with triple-digit growth), Meta Platforms (undervalued advertising company with strong AI improvements and future AI product potential), and Nebius (neocloud provider experiencing 684% revenue growth year-over-year with major clients including Meta).

items.title: Is This AI Data Center Stock a Buy While the Market Panics About Oversupply?
items.summary: Vertiv, a leader in liquid cooling and critical power systems for AI data centers, has seen its stock price decline amid market concerns about potential oversupply of data center capacity in 2026-2027. However, the article argues that rising AI cluster densities and increasing demand for advanced cooling solutions support a bullish long-term outlook. The author recommends dollar-cost averaging into Vertiv rather than making a large lump-sum investment, citing its strong $15 billion order backlog and leadership position, while acknowledging cyclical risks from slower hyperscaler spending growth and supply chain constraints.

items.title: Nvidia Crushes Earnings, But AI Chip Supply Crunch Looms
items.summary: Nvidia delivered strong Q1 fiscal results with $81.6 billion in revenue (up 85% YoY) and beat earnings expectations, driven by surging data center demand. The company raised its dividend and authorized $80 billion in share buybacks. However, supply constraints on memory chips may limit future growth, with CEO Jensen Huang noting the VeraRubin system will remain supply-constrained despite strong demand.

items.title: The Trade Desk Just Waved a Red Flag, and It's not the Quarterly Results
items.summary: The Trade Desk's stock has crashed 84% since late 2024 as revenue growth has decelerated from 20%+ to just 8%, with Q1 earnings missing estimates and weak Q2 guidance. Beyond the poor financials, CEO Jeff Green's lack of accountability and dismissal of serious competitive threats from Meta, Alphabet, and Amazon is concerning. The company also lost two CFOs in six months and faces a major conflict with Publicis Group over unauthorized billing.

items.title: Why Applied Digital Stock Soared Today
items.summary: Applied Digital announced another major 15-year lease agreement worth approximately $7.5 billion with a hyperscaler for its Polaris Forge 3 data center site, covering 300 MW of AI computing capacity. The deal brings the company's total contracted lease revenue to $31 billion across four campuses, with operations expected to begin in August 2027. The stock surged 21.68% on the announcement.

items.title: Palantir Paradox—Record Numbers and a Stock That Won’t Cooperate
items.summary: Palantir Technologies posted record Q1 2026 earnings with 85% revenue growth, near-50% operating margins, and $8 billion in cash, yet the stock sold off. Long-term bull Tom Nash argues this is normal market behavior for a stock priced for perfection. He highlights Palantir's competitive moat in government AI infrastructure and chaos-management capabilities, dismissing AI disruption concerns based on strong financial metrics. Nash recommends a buy-and-hold strategy with predetermined trim rules for existing investors and slow dollar-cost averaging for new entrants.

items.title: Why Costco Stock Dropped Today
items.summary: Costco stock fell 2.6% after Kroger's new CEO announced plans for aggressive price cuts across thousands of products to compete with rivals. However, the article argues the threat is minimal, noting Kroger's plan lacks specific details and Costco ranks #5 in consumer trust compared to Kroger's #27 ranking.

items.title: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Expands with AI-Powered Shopping Guides - Insider Monkey
items.summary: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Expands with AI-Powered Shopping Guides    Insider Monkey

items.title: The Trillion-Dollar IPO Test: SpaceX and OpenAI Face Public Markets
items.summary: SpaceX filed its S-1 prospectus on May 20, 2026, targeting a Nasdaq debut on June 12 at a reported valuation of $1.75-$2.0 trillion with a potential $75 billion raise. OpenAI is preparing to confidentially file for an IPO targeting September 2026 at above $1 trillion valuation. Both offerings represent unprecedented scale in capital markets history, with combined equity supply potentially exceeding $135 billion. The key investor debate centers on whether public markets will capitalize trillion-dollar AI infrastructure narratives, given SpaceX's consolidated net loss and OpenAI's projected cash burn of $27 billion in 2026 despite strong revenue growth.

items.title: Should Amazon’s Tariff Lawsuit And Amazon Now AI Push Require Action From Amazon.com (AMZN) Investors? - Yahoo Finance
items.summary: Should Amazon’s Tariff Lawsuit And Amazon Now AI Push Require Action From Amazon.com (AMZN) Investors?    Yahoo Finance

items.title: Nvidia Options Traders Bet On A Big Move As Earnings Loom
items.summary: Nvidia is set to report earnings with options traders positioning for significant volatility. Consensus estimates call for $1.76 EPS on $78.8B revenue, but investors will focus on forward guidance and the company's role in AI infrastructure. The options market is pricing in a ~5.5% post-earnings move, with bullish positioning dominating as call demand significantly outpaces put demand.

items.title: The AI Stock That Could Quietly Double Your Retirement Savings Over the Next Decade
items.summary: Nvidia is positioned as a generational investment opportunity that could double retirement savings over the next decade. The chipmaker benefits from exponential computing demand driven by agentic AI, which requires 1,000 times more tokens per task than standard chatbot queries. With a $5.3 trillion market cap, 73% year-over-year revenue growth, and dominant market share in AI processors, Nvidia maintains substantial pricing power despite competition. The company's continued R&D investments in next-generation chips like Vera Rubin position it to remain the dominant supplier of AI infrastructure as tech giants accelerate capital expenditures.

items.title: Amazon's Mispriced Trifecta: Logistics, Ads, And The Capex Inversion (NASDAQ:AMZN) - Seeking Alpha
items.summary: Amazon's Mispriced Trifecta: Logistics, Ads, And The Capex Inversion (NASDAQ:AMZN)    Seeking Alpha

items.title: Nvidia's Earnings Are Hours Away. Here Are 3 Things to Watch.
items.summary: Nvidia reports fiscal Q1 earnings with three key items to watch: management's guidance for Q2 (critical indicator of AI demand momentum), progress on the Vera Rubin next-generation chip platform, and any updates on China market opportunities following recent export restriction easing. The stock trades at a P/E of 45, up 19% year-to-date, with hyperscaler capex spending continuing to accelerate.

items.title: Nasdaq Composite Has a Rough Morning; Dow Holds Up Better (Barely)
items.summary: The Nasdaq fell 0.86% on Tuesday as rising Treasury yields (30-year at 5.19%, highest since 2008) and geopolitical tensions weighed on markets. Tech giants Alphabet and Amazon led declines despite positive news, with Alphabet announcing a $5 billion AI infrastructure joint venture with Blackstone. The author advises long-term investors to stay calm, characterizing the volatility as market noise rather than fundamental deterioration.

items.title: SaintQuant Launches Institutional-Grade AI Trading Platform as Goldman Sachs Declares AI the New "Defensive Trade"
items.summary: Goldman Sachs has identified AI as Wall Street's new defensive trade amid inflation and economic uncertainty, with hyperscalers projected to spend $755 billion on capex in 2026. SaintQuant launches a no-code AI trading platform to democratize algorithmic trading for retail investors, offering a $99 free trial and $7 bonus to participate in the institutional AI investment trend.

items.title: AMD Takes A Piece, Amazon Doubles Down: The New Marvell Mania
items.summary: Amazon increased its stake in Marvell Technology by purchasing 170,924 shares in Q1, bringing its total position to 396,352 shares worth $39.26 million. This move follows AMD's recent $6.5 million Marvell investment, signaling that major AI players are taking financial exposure to companies supporting their custom silicon and AI infrastructure buildout. Marvell, a key supplier and co-designer for Amazon's Trainium chips, is increasingly viewed as a levered AI infrastructure play tied to hyperscaler capital expenditure.

items.title: Trump Ally Ron DeSantis Calls Out Big Tech's Hypocrisy On AI Layoffs And 'Cheap Labor' H-1B Visas: 'Not Hard To See Why...'
items.summary: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis criticized major tech companies for contradictory practices: automating white-collar jobs through AI while simultaneously advocating for H-1B visas to hire cheaper foreign labor. DeSantis, aligned with the Trump administration, has taken steps to restrict H-1B usage in Florida public universities. Federal data shows H-1B visa applications at major tech firms have sharply declined in late 2025 amid stricter immigration rules and rising layoffs.

items.title: Nvidia Earnings on May 20: What History Tells Us About Nvidia Stock's Post-Earnings-Release Moves
items.summary: Nvidia is scheduled to report Q1 fiscal 2027 earnings on May 20, 2026. The company has beaten Wall Street earnings estimates in 21 of the past 23 quarters (91.3%), with guidance suggesting 77-80% revenue growth driven by massive capex increases from hyperscalers. However, historical data shows no significant correlation between earnings beat magnitude and immediate stock price movement, suggesting long-term fundamentals matter more than quarterly surprises.

items.title: Could Cerebras Be the Next Nvidia?
items.summary: Cerebras Systems surged 68% on its IPO debut, raising $5.5 billion as the year's largest IPO, with claims its AI chip outperforms Nvidia's GPUs by up to 15 times in inference speed. However, the company faces significant challenges including heavy customer concentration (62% revenue from one customer), limited customer base compared to Nvidia's decades of market dominance, and historical data suggesting new IPO stocks underperform in the long term.

items.title: This Small Crypto Miner Pivoting to AI Infrastructure Is Up by 75% This Year. Is It a Buy?
items.summary: Keel Infrastructure, a former crypto miner pivoting to AI infrastructure, has gained 75% year-to-date and is approaching a $2.5 billion market cap. With a 2.2 gigawatt capacity pipeline and plans to sign three major leases in 2026, the company is positioned to generate significant recurring revenue from tech giants, though it still lacks major deals compared to competitors like Cipher Mining and Iren.

items.title: UPS Could Thrive in a Post-Amazon World
items.summary: UPS is executing a strategic pivot to reduce Amazon deliveries by 50% and shift toward higher-margin customers, including small and medium-sized businesses and healthcare products. Despite near-term headwinds from trade tensions and macro weakness, the company is showing incremental improvements with rising SMB volumes and record healthcare revenue. With a forward P/E of 14x and 6.6% dividend yield, UPS presents a buying opportunity as its turnaround gains momentum.

items.title: Elon Musk Loses OpenAI Lawsuit-What The Trial Revealed About AI Investing
items.summary: A federal jury dismissed Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, ruling that the case was filed too late under statute of limitations. The verdict clears major legal obstacles for OpenAI's anticipated IPO and secures Microsoft's $228.3 billion stake in the company. The trial revealed OpenAI's 35x forward revenue multiple and $24 billion annualized revenue run rate, positioning it for a potential $1 trillion public listing.

items.title: Is Now the Time to Buy Forgotten FAANG Stock Netflix?
items.summary: Netflix, once a FAANG darling, has maintained steady growth with 12.6% annualized revenue growth over five years and 16% revenue growth in 2025. The company continues to raise prices while retaining customers, is investing in AI to reduce production costs, and achieved an 83% year-over-year net income increase in Q1 2026 (boosted by a $2.8B merger termination fee). Despite being edged out of the Magnificent Seven, Netflix's combination of top-line growth, margin expansion, and strategic AI investments suggest potential upside at its current 28 P/E ratio.

items.title: Meta’s Selloff Looks Mispriced Against Its AI-Driven Earnings Power
items.summary: Meta's stock fell 8% post-Q1 earnings despite delivering 33% revenue growth, 62% EPS growth, and strong AI-driven monetization metrics including a $20B value optimization suite and 1.6% conversion rate improvements. The article argues the selloff is mispriced, as Meta trades at 19x forward P/E (14% below 5-year average) and 11x forward operating cash flow versus Alphabet's 22x, despite growing 50% faster. The capex increase to $125-145B is attributed to component costs rather than new projects, and the AI infrastructure is directly generating measurable returns unlike the metaverse spending era.

items.title: Dan Loeb Dumps Microsoft, Slashes Nvidia And Rail Stocks In Sweeping Q1 Portfolio Overhaul
items.summary: Hedge fund billionaire Daniel Loeb's Third Point LLC significantly reshuffled its portfolio in Q1 2026, completely exiting positions in Microsoft, Chipotle, Constellation Energy, Alibaba, Spotify, and Thermo Fisher Scientific. The fund also dramatically reduced stakes in Nvidia (from 2.95M to 190K shares), Amazon, Taiwan Semiconductor, and railroad stocks Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern.

items.title: Warren Buffett’s Cash Hoard: Why $397 Billion Sits on the Sidelines
items.summary: Berkshire Hathaway's cash hoard has reached a record $397.4 billion under new CEO Greg Abel, growing from $373 billion left by Buffett at year-end 2025. The massive cash position reflects disciplined value investing amid historically expensive market valuations, with Berkshire selling $172.93 billion in equities since 2022 while earning 4-5% on Treasury bills. However, this conservative stance cost shareholders approximately $125 billion in foregone gains compared to S&P 500 returns over 2023-2025, particularly missing mega-cap growth stocks like Nvidia, Microsoft, and Meta.

items.title: Nvidia Earnings Could Decide Whether the AI Trade Still Holds
items.summary: Markets faced mixed signals as geopolitical tensions eased on Iran sanctions reports, causing oil prices to reverse sharply lower. Bond yields surged globally on inflation concerns, with the 10-year Treasury hitting 4.631%. Nvidia's Wednesday earnings are positioned as the most critical event for the AI capital expenditure cycle, with options pricing a potential 7% move. Key corporate actions include NextEra-Dominion merger announcement, Berkshire Hathaway's portfolio restructuring under new CEO Abel, and Regeneron's failed Phase 3 trial.

items.title: Warren Buffett's Successor, Greg Abel, Just Sold 16 Stocks, but Piled Into an AI Titan That's Now a Top-5 Position for Berkshire Hathaway
items.summary: Greg Abel, Warren Buffett's successor as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, exited 16 stock positions in Q1 2026 citing expensive market valuations. However, he significantly increased Berkshire's stake in Alphabet to a top-5 holding, more than tripling the position to approximately $23 billion. The move reflects Abel's focus on value investing and Alphabet's strong fundamentals including its search monopoly and rapidly growing Google Cloud business with AI integration.

items.title: Jim Cramer Reveals He’s Been A Fan Of Amazon.com (AMZN) CEO Before Anyone Else Was - Yahoo Finance
items.summary: Jim Cramer Reveals He’s Been A Fan Of Amazon.com (AMZN) CEO Before Anyone Else Was    Yahoo Finance

items.title: The AI Stock With a 10-Year Head Start That Wall Street Still Hasn't Fully Priced In
items.summary: Alphabet has a significant competitive advantage in AI infrastructure through its custom tensor processing units (TPUs), developed over a decade ago. The company's eighth-generation TPUs now offer specialized variants for training and inference, enabling lower-cost AI operations. Alphabet is beginning to sell these chips directly through partners, creating a new revenue stream. With a forward P/E of 28, the stock may still have upside potential as the market hasn't fully valued its integrated AI capabilities.

items.title: Consumer Tech News (May 11-15): Trump–Xi Talk Fails to Deliver Chip Deal, Elon Musk vs. OpenAI Trail & More
items.summary: Global semiconductor stocks retreated after Trump and Xi's summit failed to announce major chip agreements or breakthroughs on NVIDIA's China business. Key developments include Taiwan Semiconductor's $31.28B capital spending approval for AI chip manufacturing, Meta offering free WhatsApp access to competing AI developers to resolve EU antitrust concerns, Trump Mobile delivering delayed $499 smartphones, and the Elon Musk vs. OpenAI trial concluding with closing arguments. Notable earnings included strong results from Applied Materials, Cisco, and Tower Semiconductor, while Alibaba reported mixed results and Birkenstock faced margin pressures.

items.title: 3 Reasons Why Amazon Is a Top AI Investment
items.summary: Amazon is positioned as a top AI investment due to three key factors: its custom AI chips (Trainium series) are experiencing triple-digit revenue growth and replacing GPUs similar to how its Graviton chips replaced Intel CPUs; massive capital expenditures ($200 billion this year) are backed by major client commitments and will generate recurring revenue long-term; and its valuation is reasonable compared to peers when measured by operating cash flow metrics.

items.title: Under Greg Abel, Apple Stock Looks Like It's Here to Stay in Berkshire Hathaway's Portfolio
items.summary: Under new CEO Greg Abel, Berkshire Hathaway halted its multi-quarter Apple selling trend and more than tripled its Alphabet stake in Q1 2026, signaling a shift toward technology investments. Apple reported strong fundamentals with 17% revenue growth and 22% EPS growth, though rising memory costs present a near-term risk.

items.title: NextEra Energy, Dominion In Talks To Create $400 Billion Utility Giant As Electricity Demand Surges Amid AI Boom: Report
items.summary: NextEra Energy and Dominion Energy are in talks to merge in a mostly stock-based transaction that would create a roughly $400 billion utility powerhouse. The merger would combine two of the nation's largest electricity providers to meet surging power demand from AI data centers, industrial reshoring, and electrification. Dominion's footprint in Virginia and the Carolinas would expand NextEra's reach into Northern Virginia's data center hub.

items.title: Warren Buffett's Successor, Greg Abel, Dumped Amazon and Domino's, and More Than Tripled Berkshire's Stake in a Virtual Monopoly in a Massive Portfolio Overhaul
items.summary: Greg Abel, who took over as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway on December 31, executed a major portfolio overhaul in Q1 2026. He completely exited 16 positions including Amazon, Domino's Pizza, Visa, Mastercard, and UnitedHealth Group, while more than tripling Berkshire's stake in Alphabet to approximately $23 billion. The moves signal a shift toward tech investments and fundamental bargains, marking a departure from Warren Buffett's traditional investment approach.

items.title: AI/R Compass UOL avança na aplicação de inteligência artificial em DevOps e conquista reconhecimento da Amazon Web Services
items.summary: AI/R Compass UOL, a subsidiary of AI/R specializing in Agentic AI, has achieved AWS DevOps Competency recognition from Amazon Web Services. The company integrates advanced automation, continuous monitoring, and security practices into its DevOps model. Recent projects demonstrate significant results: a financial sector client achieved 25% operational cost reduction, while a sports industry client built a scalable platform serving over 500,000 users.

items.title: Tristan Thompson Reveals How NBA Dinners Around Warriors Games Landed Him An Anthropic Investment
items.summary: NBA champion Tristan Thompson disclosed he holds equity in Anthropic (valued at ~$900 billion) through an investment made during Silicon Valley dinners around Warriors games. Thompson invested via a special purpose vehicle (SPV) pooling money from fellow athletes. The investment highlights how access to pre-IPO AI companies largely depends on insider networks, though secondary markets have emerged. Anthropic is expected to go public in 2026.

items.title: Prediction: Nvidia Will Deliver Another Blowout Earnings Report on May 20, but It Won't Move the Stock in a Big Way
items.summary: Nvidia is expected to report strong fiscal Q1 2027 earnings on May 20 with revenue growth of ~44% YoY and EPS growth of ~115% YoY, driven by continued AI infrastructure spending from major hyperscalers. However, the article predicts the stock won't see significant near-term gains due to the company's massive market cap requiring exceptional beats to move the stock, and because institutional investors likely already price in strong results.

items.title: 3 Top Stocks That Growth Investors Should Buy Now
items.summary: The article recommends three growth stocks for investors: Nvidia, Meta Platforms, and Amazon. Nvidia is highlighted for its dominant position in AI chip manufacturing with expected 72% revenue growth and massive data center opportunities through 2030. Meta is valued cheaply by price-to-operating-cash-flow metrics despite heavy AI investments and strong 33% revenue growth. Amazon is recommended primarily for its AWS cloud division, which generates 59% of operating profits while representing only 21% of revenue, with 28% year-over-year growth.

items.title: Dow Jones Rally Looks Fragile With Inflation Data Running Hot
items.summary: The Dow Jones climbed back above 50,000 driven by Cisco's strong earnings beat and guidance raise, while Nvidia gained on news of Chinese H200 chip approvals. However, the rally appears fragile as inflation data runs hot with import prices spiking 1.9% and energy costs surging, offsetting positive consumer resilience metrics. The Trump-Xi summit in Beijing provided mild sentiment support but delivered limited concrete deliverables.

items.title: Nasdaq 100 Reaches Record Highs as Nvidia Rises
items.summary: US stocks reached record highs on Thursday with the Nasdaq and S&P 500 driven by technology and chipmaker gains. Nvidia rose after reports that the US approved Chinese firms to purchase its H200 AI chip, easing trade restrictions. Cisco surged 18% following an AI-focused restructuring plan with 4,000 job cuts but strong revenue growth forecasts. Markets brushed aside inflation concerns as investors monitored the US-China summit and awaited retail sales data.

items.title: Wall Street Wants to See $78.8 Billion in Revenues from Nvidia on May 20. Can It Pass the Test?
items.summary: Nvidia is expected to report $78.8 billion in Q1 fiscal 2027 revenue (77% YoY growth) on May 20, 2026. While the company has consistently beaten expectations, investors will closely watch forward guidance for Q2 (expected $86.6B) and gross margins (targeted at 75%). The four largest customers—Amazon, Microsoft, Alphabet, and Meta—are collectively spending over $700 billion on AI infrastructure this year, supporting Nvidia's growth, though the author questions whether AI spending has gotten ahead of actual returns.

items.title: Nvidia Reports Earnings This Month, and I'm Not Buying Shares. 1 AI Stock to Buy Now Instead.
items.summary: While Nvidia continues strong growth with 73% year-over-year revenue increase and guidance for 75% growth, the article argues its premium valuation (P/E of 46) leaves little room for error. Major customers like Google, Meta, and OpenAI are developing custom AI chips through Broadcom, reducing dependence on Nvidia. Amazon's custom chip business is highlighted as a better investment opportunity, growing at triple-digit pace with a $50 billion potential annual run rate, while trading at a more reasonable 32x earnings multiple.

items.title: Tesla Stock Is Soaring. Is the $10 Trillion Robotaxi Story Finally a Buy?
items.summary: Tesla's stock has surged 26% over the past month, driven by excitement around its expanding Robotaxi autonomous ride-sharing service. However, the article questions whether the valuation is justified, noting that Robotaxi won't generate material revenue until 2027, while Tesla faces a $25 billion capital expenditure plan in 2026 and operates at single-digit margins. The author suggests waiting for a better entry price given the execution risks.

items.title: 3 Growth Stocks Long-Term Investors Should Buy in May
items.summary: The article recommends three growth stocks for long-term investors: Amazon, benefiting from accelerating AWS revenue and expanding chip and logistics businesses; Apple, leveraging its high-margin services ecosystem; and Dutch Bros, capitalizing on strong same-store sales growth and significant expansion opportunities in the restaurant sector.

items.title: The Q1 Cloud Face-off Is Over -- There Was 1 Clear Winner
items.summary: In Q1 2026, the Big Three cloud providers (Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet) all reported accelerating growth driven by AI demand. While AWS grew 28% to $37.6B and Azure grew 30% to $34.7B, Google Cloud emerged as the clear winner with 63% growth to $20B—its fastest pace since 2020. Google Cloud's AI revenue surged 800% year-over-year, and the company's backlog nearly doubled to $460B, making Alphabet a particularly compelling investment opportunity.

items.title: Where Will Amazon Stock Be in 5 Years?
items.summary: Amazon demonstrates strong momentum across AWS, AI infrastructure, retail, and advertising segments. However, the stock's recent rally has raised valuation concerns. The key question is whether current free cash flow pressure represents a warning sign or a necessary investment to build a higher-margin AI platform.

items.title: Hedge Fund Bets Big on Minerals Stock, According to Recent SEC Filing
items.summary: Long Corridor Asset Management disclosed a new position in Natural Resource Partners (NRP), purchasing 100,000 shares worth approximately $11.85 million in Q1 2026. The stake represents 3.70% of the fund's reportable assets. While NRP has shown strong 3-year performance (152% return, 36% CAGR) and significantly reduced debt, its valuation has expanded considerably with a P/S ratio of 7.7x, more than double its 10-year average, presenting mixed investment signals.

items.title: The Next Phase of the AI Chip Supercycle Is Here. 1 Growth Stock Buy Before It Is Too Late (Hint: It Isn't Intel or Broadcom)
items.summary: Arm Holdings is positioned to become a major beneficiary of the shift toward AI inference workloads, which McKinsey estimates will dominate data center computing by 2030. The company's energy-efficient chip designs and new Arm AGI CPU for agentic AI have generated over $2 billion in customer demand. Management projects $15 billion in annual revenue from the AGI CPU by fiscal 2031 and overall revenue of $25 billion, with EPS reaching $9.00, suggesting potential 51% upside if valued at current tech multiples.

items.title: Andy Jassy Just Said Something Big: Here's What It Means for Amazon's Stock
items.summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy called generative AI a 'once-in-a-generation opportunity' to justify the company's massive $200 billion capital expenditure forecast for 2026. While Jassy compares current AI investments to AWS's successful rollout in the 2000s, the article cautions that such heavy spending on data centers carries risk if anticipated demand doesn't materialize. Investors should await more tangible progress before getting excited about the strategy.

items.title: OpenAI's IPO Dream Gets Massive Boost As Microsoft Reportedly Agrees To $38 Billion Revenue Cap— Nadella Says 'Very Proud' Of Early Bet
items.summary: Microsoft and OpenAI have renegotiated their partnership terms, capping total revenue-sharing payments at $38 billion. The deal allows OpenAI greater flexibility to work with other cloud providers like Amazon and Google while Microsoft remains the primary partner. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella expressed pride in the company's early investment in OpenAI. These terms could strengthen OpenAI's appeal to investors ahead of a potential IPO by year-end.

items.title: After an 11% Drop, Is CoreWeave a Buy on the Dip or a Stock to Avoid?
items.summary: CoreWeave, a specialized AI compute provider, has dropped 11% following earnings despite doubling revenue to $2 billion. While the company boasts a $100 billion backlog and strong demand for GPU services, investor concerns about heavy debt reliance and increased capital spending forecasts ($31 billion) have pressured the stock. The article suggests CoreWeave could be attractive for aggressive investors betting on AI growth, but cautious investors may prefer established cloud leaders.

items.title: This AI Stock Is Crushing Nvidia in 2026. It's Still a Buy After Soaring 240% This Year, According to Wall Street.
items.summary: DigitalOcean has surged 240% in 2026 by pivoting to AI services, significantly outperforming Nvidia's 15% gain. While Nvidia maintains dominance in AI infrastructure with 90% market share in accelerators and strong competitive moats, DigitalOcean's AI-Native Cloud platform targets the growing inference market. However, at 81x earnings, DigitalOcean appears expensive despite strong guidance, while Nvidia at 45x earnings with 53% annual earnings growth through 2028 offers better value for patient investors.

items.title: 3 Top Stocks to Buy in May
items.summary: The article recommends three stocks for May 2026: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC), Amazon (AMZN), and Lemonade (LMND). TSMC benefits from strong AI chip demand with 41% YoY revenue growth and 66.2% gross margin. Amazon shows accelerated growth across AWS, e-commerce, and advertising, with AI driving triple-digit revenue growth. Lemonade, an AI-powered insurance disruptor, achieved 32% YoY IFP growth and is expected to reach profitability by year-end.

items.title: Prediction: AI Infrastructure Stocks Will Crush the S&P 500 in 2026
items.summary: Tech giants are investing nearly $700 billion in AI infrastructure build-out in 2026 to meet current customer demand. As AI moves from research into real-world applications, infrastructure companies providing chips, servers, and networking equipment are positioned for explosive growth. With attractive valuations at seven-year lows and strong revenue momentum, AI infrastructure stocks are predicted to outperform the broader S&P 500.

items.title: 3 Growth Stocks Down 20% to Buy Right Now
items.summary: Despite record-high stock indexes, three growth stocks trading significantly below their all-time highs present potential buying opportunities. MercadoLibre faces margin pressures and loan defaults but maintains strong 49% revenue growth. Chewy has recovered operationally with 125% operating income surge despite being down 80% from peaks. Shopify's rapid 34% revenue growth and 88% operating income increase are overshadowed by AI-related concerns, though its ecosystem moat remains defensible.

items.title: Is CoreWeave Stock a Buy on the Dip as Revenue Continues to Skyrocket?
items.summary: CoreWeave's stock fell despite reporting 112% revenue growth to $2.08 billion and a nearly $100 billion project backlog. However, the company's adjusted EPS loss widened to $1.12 (worse than expected $0.91), Q2 guidance missed consensus, and rising component costs forced the company to increase its capex budget. CoreWeave remains highly leveraged and dependent on off-the-shelf GPUs, making it vulnerable to rising chip prices.

items.title: Why Caterpillar Could Be the AI Stock of the Year
items.summary: Caterpillar, a century-old construction equipment manufacturer, is emerging as a compelling AI infrastructure play due to massive demand for data center construction from tech giants like Amazon, Alphabet, and Microsoft. The company reported a 22% revenue increase to $17.4 billion in Q1 2026, record backlogs, and a 170% stock surge over 12 months, positioning it as an overlooked but critical player in the AI physical infrastructure build-out.

items.title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy Has Good News and Bad News for Nvidia Investors
items.summary: Amazon is increasing AI chip purchases but shifting focus toward its own custom Trainium accelerators instead of Nvidia GPUs. CEO Andy Jassy revealed that AWS is deploying more Trainium chips than Nvidia chips and has a $225 billion backlog for Trainium compute. Other cloud providers like Google and Microsoft are similarly developing custom chips, threatening Nvidia's dominant market position in AI accelerators.

items.title: Has Amazon Found the Next AWS?
items.summary: Amazon has launched Amazon Supply Chain Services (ASCS), opening its logistics network to external businesses similar to how AWS operates. The service, already adopted by major corporations like Procter & Gamble and 3M, could become a high-margin profit driver. While AWS remains Amazon's strongest business segment, ASCS represents another growth avenue, though its long-term success remains uncertain.

items.title: Prediction: Amazon Will Outperform the S&P 500
items.summary: Amazon is positioned to outperform the S&P 500 in 2026 due to accelerating AWS cloud revenue growth (28% in Q1), expanding chip business ($20B run-rate), strong positioning in AI infrastructure, and underappreciated e-commerce efficiency gains through robotics and AI. The stock trades at an attractive 32x forward P/E compared to peers, offering room for growth and multiple expansion.

items.title: 3 Lessons for Transportation Industry Investors Following Spirit Airlines' Bankruptcy
items.summary: Spirit Airlines' bankruptcy provides three key lessons for transportation investors: avoid buying stocks immediately after bankruptcy emergence as companies often face recurring financial troubles, be cautious when struggling companies attempt major acquisitions as a last resort, and carefully monitor airline balance sheets and leverage ratios since high debt levels leave little room to weather adversity like fuel price spikes.

items.title: Amazon vs. Microsoft: Which Tech Giant Looks Better After Its Earnings Report?
items.summary: Amazon and Microsoft reported earnings on April 29, with diverging trajectories. Amazon's AWS grew 28% year-over-year with accelerating momentum, while Microsoft Cloud grew 29% but at a slower acceleration rate. Amazon achieved its highest net profit margin ever at 16.7%, driven by high-margin businesses like advertising and AI chips. Despite Microsoft's higher 38.3% net profit margin, Amazon's improving profitability and multiple growth avenues position it more favorably for long-term growth.

items.title: Is Backblaze the Next Momentum Monster?
items.summary: Backblaze (BLZE) surged 64% following Q1 earnings, driven by its strategic positioning in the AI infrastructure market as a low-cost data lake provider for the emerging Neocloud ecosystem. The company is gaining traction with a 76% YOY increase in AI customers, expanding enterprise sales under new CRO leadership, and demonstrating improving unit economics with 26% adjusted EBITDA margins. Management projects positive adjusted free cash flow in H2 2026, signaling a transition to sustainable profitability.

items.title: Nvidia Stock Has Huge Momentum. Time to Buy?
items.summary: Nvidia stock has surged 19% over the last 30 days, driven by exceptional Q4 2026 results showing 73% revenue growth and record data center revenue of $62.3 billion. Management guidance projects 77% year-over-year growth for Q1 2026. While the P/E ratio of 43 appears reasonable relative to growth, significant risks exist including competition from customers developing their own chips, China market uncertainty, and questions about sustainability of hyperscaler spending.

items.title: Questex’s StreamTV Show 2026 Unveils Its Most Powerful Market Floor Yet - Fueled by Industry Giants with Expansive, Immersive Market Floor Experiences
items.summary: StreamTV Show 2026, taking place June 16-19 in Denver, will feature the largest market floor to date with major tech and advertising companies including Google, LG, and Vizio showcasing immersive brand experiences. Cineverse will anchor the event with a 40x40 booth highlighting its newly acquired companies and integrated streaming ecosystem through its Matchpoint platform.

items.title: Coinbase Valuation Depends on Lower Costs as Trading Volumes Fall
items.summary: Coinbase announced a 14% workforce reduction (700 positions) on May 5, 2026, citing AI automation as justification. The layoffs come amid a 50% Bitcoin decline from October 2025 peak ($125K to $62K) and 30% year-over-year drop in trading volumes. The restructuring targets $120M-$150M in annual operating expense savings against $50M-$60M one-time charges. Q1 2026 revenue fell 5% YoY to $1.6B despite the company holding $7.5B in cash.

items.title: Here's Why UPS Stock Is Rising and Falling in 2026
items.summary: UPS stock remains flat in 2026 despite April gains, facing headwinds from its voluntary 50% reduction in Amazon deliveries through mid-2026. While the company expects margin recovery in the second half, near-term profitability is pressured by network adjustment costs and broader package volume declines. Amazon's new logistics service (ASCS) poses an additional competitive threat to UPS's business.

items.title: Amazon, Google, Microsoft In Focus As EU Reportedly Mulls Restricting US Cloud Providers' Access To Sensitive Data
items.summary: The European Union is considering implementing regulations to restrict US cloud providers' access to sensitive government data as part of its upcoming 'Tech Sovereignty Package' to be presented on May 27. The proposals would limit but not fully exclude foreign cloud providers from handling classified public-sector information, affecting major US tech companies. This move is driven by concerns over the US Cloud Act and strained US-EU relations under the Trump administration.

items.title: Alphabet Can Become the Most Valuable Company Thanks to Google Cloud
items.summary: Alphabet is positioned to become the world's most valuable company, potentially surpassing Nvidia this year, driven by Google Cloud's impressive 63% year-over-year revenue growth and 81% net income increase in Q1. The company is demonstrating superior AI return on investment compared to competitors, with stronger profit margins and $126.8 billion in cash reserves enabling accelerated capital expenditures and market share gains. Emerging segments like Waymo and Gemini show additional long-term growth potential.

items.title: Amazon Just Announced Fantastic News for Investors: Should You Buy?
items.summary: Amazon launched Amazon Supply Chain Services, allowing third-party retailers to use its delivery and logistics network. The expansion of this existing infrastructure is expected to drive revenue growth and margin expansion. With higher-margin segments like advertising (22% YoY growth) and AWS (28% YoY growth, 35% operating margin) growing faster than the core business, Amazon's operating margin could expand from 12% to ~20% over the next few years. At a market cap of $2.9 trillion and trading at 16x forward earnings, the stock is fairly valued for a megacap with strong growth prospects.

items.title: AI Stocks Were Just Put to the Test. Here's How They Fared.
items.summary: AI stocks faced significant headwinds in early 2026 due to valuation concerns, with the S&P 500 Shiller CAPE ratio reaching 40 (matching dot-com bubble levels), and geopolitical uncertainty from the Iran war. However, AI stocks have rebounded strongly as tech giants demonstrated that their massive AI infrastructure spending is justified by solid customer commitments and strong revenue growth. Companies like Amazon are monetizing their $200 billion AI investments through committed customer deals, proving the long-term viability of the AI investment thesis.

items.title: Vertiv: The Backlog is Massive, the Margin of Safety is Not
items.summary: Vertiv (VRT), a leader in data center power and thermal infrastructure for AI, has surged 300% in 12 months with a massive $15 billion backlog and improving unit economics. However, the stock trades at a 50.9x forward P/E multiple with only a 1.9% FCF yield, leaving minimal margin of safety despite strong fundamentals and growth prospects. The valuation assumes sustained high growth and successful services transformation, with execution risks from project delays and financing constraints.

items.title: This "Magnificent Seven" Stock Is the Cheapest of Them All. Is It a Buy Right Now?
items.summary: Meta Platforms is trading at the lowest valuation among the Magnificent Seven stocks at 19.8x forward P/E ratio. Despite a 6% decline this year, the article argues the stock is undervalued given strong Q1 advertising revenue growth (33% YoY) and strategic AI investments. Investor skepticism centers on Meta's increased capital expenditure plans ($125-145 billion), though the author contends this spending is necessary to remain competitive in AI infrastructure.

items.title: Did Amazon Just Give This Logisitcs Stock a No-Brainer Buying Opportunity?
items.summary: GXO Logistics stock plunged 18% after Amazon announced its new supply chain services business, but CEO Patrick Kelleher dismissed the threat, arguing that GXO's customized, specialized logistics solutions serve a different market than Amazon's pre-existing infrastructure offering. GXO reported strong Q1 earnings with 10.8% revenue growth and raised full-year guidance, with the CEO believing the stock sell-off presents a buying opportunity.

items.title: Why Amazon Might Be the Real Winner of the Microsoft and OpenAI Partnership Shakeup
items.summary: Microsoft's exclusive license to OpenAI's models has ended, allowing Amazon Web Services (AWS) to now offer OpenAI models to its customers. Combined with Amazon's $50 billion investment in OpenAI announced two months prior, this restructuring positions AWS to strengthen its competitive advantage in the cloud market. AWS, which generates 59% of Amazon's operating income despite only 20% of revenue, could see accelerated growth as it offers customers more AI model options.

items.title: Alphabet Stock Gains On Report Of Massive Anthropic Cloud, Chip Deal
items.summary: Alphabet stock rose 1.35-1.40% to a new 52-week high after a report revealed Anthropic committed to spend $200 billion with Google Cloud over five years. The deal reportedly accounts for over 40% of Google's disclosed revenue backlog and validates Alphabet's cloud and AI infrastructure strategy, potentially easing concerns about Google Cloud's competitive position against Amazon and Microsoft.

items.title: Apple, Amazon, TSMC Top List Of Earnings Triple Plays As Beat-And-Raise Stocks Surge
items.summary: Apple, Amazon, and TSMC are among the largest companies delivering earnings 'triple plays' in Q1 2026—beating EPS and revenue estimates while raising forward guidance. According to Bespoke Investment Group, 66 triple plays have occurred since mid-April, more than double last year's 30. Triple-play stocks averaged an 8.6% one-day gain after reporting, significantly above the five-year average of 5%, with 24 stocks gaining 10% or more. The trend spans multiple sectors including technology, healthcare, industrials, and consumer stocks.

items.title: Oracle Stock Is Still Down Year to Date, As Many AI Stocks Soar. Here's What's Going On.
items.summary: Oracle's stock has underperformed other AI stocks despite strong quarterly results and a massive $553 billion contracted backlog, largely driven by a $300 billion OpenAI deal. The company is taking on significant debt to fund AI infrastructure buildout, and investor concerns about OpenAI's ability to meet revenue targets and fund future commitments have weighed on the stock. Unlike competitors funding buildouts from internal cash flow, Oracle is leveraging debt to lock in long-dated compute contracts with uncertain economics.

items.title: 1 Update Amazon CEO Andy Jassy Just Said That Every Artificial Intelligence (AI) Investor Should Hear
items.summary: Amazon reported strong Q1 results with 17% sales growth and 30% operating income increase. AWS accelerated growth to 28% YoY, driven by AI demand. CEO Jassy emphasized the company's $200 billion 2026 capex investment in infrastructure (land, power, buildings, chips, servers) is essential for AI expansion, typically monetized 6-24 months after spending. The article highlights investment opportunities in AI supply chain companies providing critical components to hyperscalers.

items.title: Tesla, Meta, Microsoft Investors May Need A 'Self-Destruct' Switch, Not Blind Faith
items.summary: An independent market commentator argues that investors in major AI stocks like Tesla, Meta, and Microsoft should establish predetermined conditions to abandon their investment thesis when evidence turns against it. The article warns that powerful AI narratives have hardened into doctrine, with valuations heavily dependent on distant future assumptions. It emphasizes that massive capital expenditures don't automatically create competitive moats and that investors need clear metrics—such as deployment milestones, revenue conversion, and unit economics—to validate their convictions before belief overtakes analysis.

items.title: Prediction: The Best Artificial Intelligence (AI) Growth Stocks on the Nasdaq Haven't Peaked Yet. Here's What I'm Buying.
items.summary: The article argues that AI growth stocks focused on infrastructure rather than headline names offer compelling opportunities. Credo Technology Group dominates the Active Electrical Cables market with 202% YoY revenue growth, while UiPath has repositioned itself as an enterprise agentic AI execution layer and achieved GAAP profitability for the first time. Both companies benefit from hyperscaler AI infrastructure spending but face execution risks from customer concentration and competitive pressure from larger vendors.

items.title: Meta and Alphabet Will Spend a Combined $335 Billion This Year. Don't Expect Their ROI to Be the Same.
items.summary: Alphabet and Meta are increasing AI capital expenditures to $335 billion combined in 2026, with Alphabet spending $190 billion and Meta $145 billion. However, investors are responding differently to their strategies: Alphabet has a clear AI monetization plan with surging Google Cloud revenue (up 63%) and growing Gemini Enterprise users, while Meta lacks a precise plan for ROI despite doubling its capex spending. Meta's stock fell 10% while Alphabet's rose over 10% following the announcements.

items.title: Roundhill Files for 'Magnificent Seven Plus' ETF With AI, Space Twist
items.summary: Roundhill Investments has filed to launch the Roundhill Magnificent Seven Plus ETF (ticker: MAGP), which would combine exposure to the dominant mega-cap tech stocks (the 'Magnificent Seven') with newer AI and space companies including Broadcom, Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX. The fund remains subject to regulatory approval, with expense ratio and launch date to be announced.

items.title: Big-Tech Earnings: Google and Meta’s Results Support Broadcom’s Outlook
items.summary: Google and Meta reported strong earnings with significant AI capital expenditure increases, benefiting their semiconductor partner Broadcom. Google's cloud business grew 63% with a nearly doubled backlog, while Meta's advertising business accelerated. Both companies signaled continued or increased spending on AI infrastructure in 2027, supporting demand for Broadcom's custom chips.

items.title: Stock Market Today: Oil Jumps 5%, S&P 500 Drops As Iran Strikes UAE Port
items.summary: U.S. stocks fell Monday as an Iranian drone strike on a UAE oil facility sent Brent crude above $114 a barrel, raising inflation concerns and expectations of a potential Fed rate hike by March 2027. The S&P 500 dropped 0.5%, the Dow fell 1.0%, and the Nasdaq 100 declined 0.7%. Energy stocks rallied while transportation, logistics, and rate-sensitive sectors suffered significant losses. Defense stocks gained on Pentagon spending narratives, while software and crypto-related equities found strength.

items.title: Here's Why GXO Logistics Shares Slumped (Hint: It's Amazon Related)
items.summary: Amazon's launch of Amazon Supply Chain Services (ASCS) caused GXO Logistics shares to drop nearly 13% as the company now offers freight, distribution, fulfillment, and parcel shipping to enterprise customers—a market segment GXO traditionally serves. While Amazon's move threatens GXO's business outlook, the company's more complex contract logistics offerings may be less impacted, and the announcement could raise awareness about logistics outsourcing opportunities.

items.title: Earnings Strength Moves Into Focus as the Busiest Week of Q1 Season Begins
items.summary: The S&P 500 is on track for its sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit earnings growth at 15.1%, driven by a 46% expansion in Information Technology. The Magnificent Seven tech companies reported strong revenue growth, but investor reactions were mixed based on capital expenditure guidance. With 63% of S&P 500 companies having reported, 84% beat EPS estimates and 81% beat revenue expectations. The busiest week of Q1 2026 earnings season begins with 3,213 companies set to report, including major names like Palantir, Disney, Uber, McDonald's, and AMD.

items.title: Prediction: Amazon Stock Will Soon Join the $3 Trillion Club
items.summary: Amazon is positioned to become the fifth company to reach $3 trillion market cap, needing only a 4% gain from current levels. The company reported strong Q1 2026 results with 17% YoY sales growth, triple-digit AI revenue growth, and expansion across e-commerce, AWS, advertising, and its new satellite broadband service Amazon Leo.

items.title: 4 Reasons Alphabet's Cloud Growth Outpaced Its Larger Rivals
items.summary: Google Cloud achieved 63% revenue growth in Q1 2026, significantly outpacing Microsoft Azure (40%) and Amazon AWS (28%). The outperformance is driven by four key factors: strong adoption of Google Gemini AI models with 800% yearly growth in cloud gen AI revenue, development of custom Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) reducing Nvidia dependency, massive data advantages from Google Search and other services enabling superior AI model training, and a growing backlog of nearly $460 billion reflecting strong AI-native project demand.

items.title: Meta AI Capex (Up 7%) Is a Bad Surprise -- Could This Be the Next Metaverse?
items.summary: Meta Platforms reported higher-than-expected AI capital expenditure guidance for 2026 ($125-145 billion vs. prior $115-135 billion), causing its stock to drop 8.6%. Unlike competitors Alphabet and Amazon, Meta lacks a clear monetization strategy for its massive AI investments, raising concerns it may repeat its failed Metaverse experiment.

items.title: The Comeback Kid? Where Will Intel Be in 1 Year?
items.summary: Intel shows signs of recovery with strong Q1 2026 results, including 7.2% year-over-year revenue growth to $13.6 billion and improved gross margins. The company's strategic pivot toward semiconductor foundry services is gaining traction, with a major manufacturing deal signed with Amazon and ongoing talks with Google. Intel is also part of a $100 billion U.S. chip factory expansion plan, suggesting the company may have more competitive strength than previously thought.

items.title: Nvidia Rival Cerebras Reloads IPO Bid With $3.5 Billion Goal
items.summary: Cerebras Systems is pursuing an IPO to raise up to $3.5 billion, planning to offer 28 million shares priced between $115-$125 each on Nasdaq under ticker 'CBRS'. The company has received strong investor demand exceeding $10 billion and recently secured an $850 million credit facility. CEO Andrew Feldman claims Cerebras hardware enables AI models to run faster than NVIDIA's systems, positioning the company as a direct competitor in the AI chip sector.

items.title: Apple Just Gave Investors a $100 Billion Reason to Rethink the AI Spending Race
items.summary: Apple authorized an additional $100 billion share repurchase program while maintaining disciplined capital expenditures of only $4.3 billion in the first half of fiscal 2026. Unlike competitors investing hundreds of billions in AI infrastructure, Apple pursues a less capital-intensive edge-based AI strategy. The company reported strong fiscal Q2 results with 17% revenue growth, 22% EPS growth, and generated $82 billion in operating cash flow, returning $45 billion to shareholders through dividends and buybacks.

items.title: GameStop's Cohen Threatens Proxy War In High-Stakes eBay Bid
items.summary: GameStop has submitted an unsolicited $56 billion bid to acquire eBay at $125 per share (46% premium), with Ryan Cohen threatening a proxy fight if rejected. The proposal includes $2 billion in planned cost reductions and leveraging GameStop's 1,600 retail locations. eBay shares surged 9% to a new 52-week high in premarket trading, while GameStop fell 3%.

items.title: This Is My Favorite AI Chip Stock (By Far) -- and It's Not Nvidia, Intel, or Broadcom
items.summary: Amazon's custom silicon chip business has reached a $20 billion annual revenue run rate with 40% sequential growth, positioning it as one of the top three data center chip businesses globally. CEO Andy Jassy stated the business could generate $50 billion annually if operated as a standalone company. With major customers like Anthropic and OpenAI committed to multi-gigawatt capacity and next-generation chips nearly fully subscribed, Amazon offers AI hardware exposure without pure-play chip valuations, though risks include elevated capital spending pressuring free cash flow.

items.title: This Often Underappreciated Growth Stock Is Holding Its Own Against Giants Amazon and Alphabet. Time to Buy?
items.summary: Roku reported strong Q1 2026 results with 28% platform revenue growth and crossed 100 million streaming households globally. While profitability improved significantly and management raised full-year guidance, the stock's 60x P/E valuation leaves little margin for safety. The company faces intense competition from tech giants Amazon and Alphabet in the streaming and advertising space, and some business segments show weakness.

items.title: Alphabet Is Outgrowing Amazon and Microsoft Where It Matters Most -- Why This Makes Alphabet Stock a Buy
items.summary: Alphabet's Google Cloud achieved 63% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026, significantly outpacing Microsoft Azure's 40% and Amazon AWS's 28% growth. With a nearly doubled backlog of $460 billion and expanding operating margins, Google Cloud is positioned as the fastest-growing cloud platform during the AI infrastructure buildout, though Alphabet's increased capital expenditure guidance to $180-190 billion introduces execution risk.

items.title: Major Hyperscalers Just Reported Earnings. Nvidia Was The Winner
items.summary: Following earnings reports from Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet, all three major cloud providers announced massive AI capital expenditure commitments, signaling strong continued demand for Nvidia's AI chips. Despite competition from Amazon's custom chips and concentration risks, Nvidia remains well-positioned with its powerful hardware, CUDA platform moat, and upcoming innovations.

items.title: Bull vs. Bear: Is Intel Stock a Buy or Sell?
items.summary: Intel stock has surged over 350% in the past year, driven by strong demand for data center CPUs amid the AI infrastructure build-out. However, the author argues the stock has moved too far too fast, trading at a forward P/E of 86x with only 7% YoY revenue growth. While Intel benefits from a shift toward CPU-heavy AI inference workloads, increasing competition from AMD, Nvidia, Arm, Amazon, and Alphabet, combined with the unprofitable foundry business, suggests taking profits rather than buying at current valuations.

items.title: Amazon Just Proved It's No Longer an AI Underdog
items.summary: Amazon's AWS division delivered 28% revenue growth in Q1 2026, its fastest growth rate among all business segments, while generating 59% of the company's total operating income despite representing only 21% of sales. AWS is experiencing triple-digit growth in custom AI chips and has secured major AI companies like OpenAI and Anthropic as customers. With Amazon investing $200 billion in capital expenditures in 2026, the company is positioning itself as a major player in the AI infrastructure space.

items.title: Meta Buys Robotics Startup Assured Robot Intelligence To Power Humanoid Push As $5 Trillion Market Race Heats Up
items.summary: Meta acquired Assured Robot Intelligence (ARI), a humanoid robotics startup, to bolster its Superintelligence Labs division's physical AI capabilities. The acquisition reflects intense competition in the humanoid robotics sector, with Goldman Sachs projecting a $38 billion market by 2035 and Morgan Stanley estimating $5 trillion by 2050. ARI co-founder Lerrel Pinto previously co-founded Fauna Robotics, which Amazon acquired in March.

items.title: Mag 7 Just Committed $710 Billion To AI Capex
items.summary: The Magnificent Seven companies committed $710 billion to AI capital expenditure in 2026, with Amazon ($200B), Microsoft ($190B), Google ($185B), and Meta ($135B) all raising guidance. The market rewarded companies converting CapEx into current revenue (Apple, Amazon, Google) while punishing those with delayed monetization (Meta, Microsoft). The Fed held rates steady but showed historic division with an 8-4 dissent split, raising questions about future monetary policy direction.

items.title: Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft, Meta, and Apple Just Reported Earnings. I Think This Was the Best Report of Them All.
items.summary: Five Magnificent Seven tech giants reported strong quarterly earnings, but Apple stood out with accelerating revenue growth (17% YoY) and expanding high-margin services (16% growth) while avoiding massive AI infrastructure spending. Unlike peers committing $125-200 billion in capex, Apple spent only $4.3 billion in the first half of fiscal 2026, positioning it as the most attractive investment among the group.

items.title: Stock Market Today: Apple Earnings Power S&P 500, Nasdaq To Records; Trump Hits EU
items.summary: U.S. equities reached all-time highs on Friday as Apple's blowout Q2 earnings powered a broad technology rally. The S&P 500 advanced 0.7% to 7,262, while the Nasdaq 100 rose 1.1% to 27,743. Apple surged 5.1% on strong revenue and guidance, while software stocks like Atlassian, Twilio, and Reddit soared on earnings beats. Energy stocks declined as crude oil fell over 3%. Some mega-cap earnings disappointed, including Amgen and Roblox, which posted significant declines.

items.title: Microsoft, Amazon On Watch ― Goldman Sounds Alarm On 90% Cloud Cash Burn
items.summary: Goldman Sachs warns that major cloud infrastructure companies are spending 90% of their operating cash flow on AI capex, with consensus estimates jumping $130 billion to $670 billion for 2026. Microsoft's capex rose 84% YoY to $31.9B in Q3 with $190B guided for 2026, while Amazon's capex surged 107% YoY to $43.2B in Q1 with $200B planned for 2026. This unsustainable spending model threatens buybacks and dividends as companies shift toward bond issuance.

items.title: Warren Buffett Went Out With a Bang by Selling 77% of His Amazon Stake and Piling Into a Virtual Monopoly That's Soared 13,600% Since Its IPO
items.summary: Warren Buffett retired as Berkshire Hathaway CEO on December 31, 2025, passing leadership to Greg Abel. Before stepping down, Buffett sold 77% of Berkshire's Amazon stake (worth ~$1.7 billion) due to valuation concerns and Amazon's aggressive AI spending plans. Conversely, he invested over $4 billion in Alphabet, viewing Google's dominant search engine as a virtual monopoly with strong competitive advantages and an attractive valuation relative to Amazon.

items.title: While "Magnificent Seven" Companies Pour Hundreds of Billions into AI Infrastructure, Apple Continues to Execute the Classic Shareholder-Friendly Playbook
items.summary: Apple reported Q2 2026 earnings that beat estimates, raising its quarterly dividend by 4% to $0.27 per share and authorizing a $100 billion share repurchase program. While other 'Magnificent Seven' companies like Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, and Meta are investing heavily in AI infrastructure with capex exceeding $100 billion annually, Apple continues its traditional shareholder-friendly approach, having repurchased $37 billion in stock and distributed $7.7 billion in dividends in the first half of fiscal 2026.

items.title: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones Roars As Industrials Rescue Wall Street From Meta-Led Tech Slide
items.summary: The Dow Jones surged 1.5% driven by industrial and healthcare stocks, while mega-cap tech faced pressure from concerns over AI capital spending. Meta and Microsoft declined on capex worries, but Alphabet gained on enterprise AI wins. Industrials like Caterpillar, Quanta Services, and Carrier rallied on data-center infrastructure demand. The S&P 500 gained 0.5% while the Nasdaq 100 remained flat.

items.title: This Unstoppable Dividend Stock Could Be the Only Retailer I'd Hold Through the Next Market Crash.
items.summary: Walmart is highlighted as the only retail stock worth holding through market downturns due to its 53-year dividend increase streak (Dividend King status), strong historical performance (3,240% stock gain over 30 years), and resilient business model. The company is expected to grow revenue and EPS at 5% and 10% CAGRs respectively through 2029, driven by e-commerce profitability, advertising expansion, supply chain automation, and international growth.

items.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg Just Delivered Great News for Nvidia and Micron Investors
items.summary: Meta raised its capital expenditure forecast to $125-145 billion (from $115-135 billion), citing higher component costs, particularly memory pricing. This signals continued strong AI spending across hyperscalers, with Google Cloud growing 63%, Azure up 39%, and AWS at 28%. Nvidia and Micron are positioned to benefit from increased capex and higher chip prices, which improve margins. The commentary suggests the AI investment boom is still in early stages with several more years of growth expected.

items.title: The 'Prove It' Quarter: How Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, And Alphabet Backed AI Capex With Real Run Rates
items.summary: Major tech hyperscalers Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet demonstrated in their latest earnings that massive AI capital expenditures are generating substantial real revenue, not speculation. The companies reported combined AI revenue run rates exceeding $100 billion annually, backed by signed customer commitments totaling hundreds of billions. The next phase will focus on autonomous 'agentic' AI systems that execute complex tasks.

items.title: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones, S&P 500 Futures Gain As Trump Weighs 'Short And Powerful' Iran Strikes— Alphabet, Meta, Amazon In Focus (UPDATED)
items.summary: U.S. stock futures showed mixed performance on Thursday as the Fed maintained interest rates at 3.50%-3.75%. Tech stocks led gains with Amazon, Alphabet, and Apple posting strong earnings results, while Microsoft and Meta raised capital expenditure guidance despite stock declines. Geopolitical tensions over potential Iran strikes and persistent inflation concerns tempered broader market sentiment.

items.title: Amazon's AWS Bets Big On Agentic AI To Reinvent Software— CEO Matt Garman Says 'Everything Is Going To Be Remade'
items.summary: AWS CEO Matt Garman announced major AI initiatives including Amazon Quick, a desktop assistant for productivity tasks, and new Connect applications for healthcare, hiring, and supply chain. AWS partnered with OpenAI to offer GPT and Codex models, and launched Connect Talent for AI-driven hiring automation. Garman stated that agentic AI is driving a massive reinvention of software, with AWS expanding AI infrastructure investments despite higher capital spending.

items.title: Stock Market Today, April 29: Mixed Reactions as Big Tech Earnings Beat Expectations
items.summary: On April 29, 2026, major stock indices remained flat as the Fed held rates unchanged amid elevated inflation concerns. Big Tech earnings after hours showed mixed results: Alphabet and Amazon gained on strong performance, while Meta and Microsoft fell. The market is focused on AI spending pressures, with tech companies' combined capex exceeding $650 billion as they race to dominate the AI market.

items.title: The Best Growth Stocks to Buy Right Now
items.summary: CoreWeave and Nebius are highlighted as compelling AI infrastructure growth stocks despite near-term market challenges. CoreWeave has scaled to 43 data centers with 250,000+ Nvidia GPUs, offering 35x faster and 80% cheaper AI processing than AWS and Azure, with revenue projected to reach $36.7B by 2028. Nebius, formerly Yandex, provides full-stack AI infrastructure services and saw 351% revenue growth in 2025, with projections to reach $10.1B by 2027. Both companies remain unprofitable but show strong growth trajectories.

items.title: Oil Surges 6%, S&P 500 Wobbles Before Fed And Tech Earnings: Stock Market Today
items.summary: U.S. stocks traded mixed on Wednesday as crude oil rallied 5-6% amid Iran tensions, pushing Treasury yields above 4.4%. The Federal Reserve is expected to hold rates steady at 3.75%, while investors await earnings from major tech companies. Big Tech stocks fell 1-2% on reports that OpenAI missed revenue targets, raising questions about AI spending sustainability.

items.title: Dally O's Memoir Explores Transformation Through the Cube of Life
items.summary: Dally Yarbrough has published 'A Night in Tucumcari,' a multidimensional memoir that uses the Cube as a central symbol to explore themes of collapse, clarity, and personal transformation. The narrative weaves together experiences from rodeo arenas, bankruptcy, betrayal, and rebirth, with the Cube's colors representing different aspects of memory and identity. The book is available on Amazon and Barnes & Noble.

items.title: Prediction: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Chip Stock Will Become the Next Nvidia by 2030
items.summary: Marvell Technology is positioned as an emerging AI chip design partner for major hyperscalers seeking to reduce dependence on Nvidia. With 18 design wins, a Google co-development partnership for custom AI chips, and the acquisition of Celestial AI's photonic interconnect technology, Marvell is expected to achieve 30%+ annual growth and reach $15 billion in revenue by fiscal 2028.

items.title: Amazon earnings beat expectations with strong cloud growth - CNBC
items.summary: Amazon earnings beat expectations with strong cloud growth    CNBC

items.title: 4 "Magnificent Seven" Companies Are About to Report Earnings -- All on the Same Day. Here's What to Watch.
items.summary: Four major tech companies -- Alphabet, Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta -- are reporting earnings on the same Wednesday after-hours session. Investors will closely monitor revenue growth and operating earnings amid record capital expenditures exceeding $600 billion combined. The key question is whether massive AI infrastructure spending translates into strong top-line growth and earnings performance, with significant market volatility expected.

items.title: What Most Investors Are Getting Wrong About Meta Platforms That Makes Right Now an Incredible Buying Opportunity
items.summary: Meta is investing heavily ($135B in capex for 2026) in AI infrastructure and talent, which is compressing operating margins in the near term. While its Muse Spark model lags competitors, the article argues investors are overlooking the massive competitive advantage Meta is building. The company's AI improvements are already driving higher engagement and ad pricing, with management expecting operating income growth despite massive spending increases. Meta's scale and ability to fine-tune AI for its own products positions it well for long-term returns in digital advertising.

items.title: Prediction: Amazon Stock Will Soar After April 29
items.summary: Amazon stock is predicted to soar following its Q1 earnings report on April 29, 2026. The company has recovered momentum and trades near record highs at a reasonable valuation of 32x forward earnings. Key catalysts include strong AI demand for AWS, which generates 60% of operating income with a $142 billion annual revenue run rate, and CEO Andy Jassy's reassurance that $200 billion in capital expenditures are linked to customer commitments and will be monetized within two years.

items.title: Why This Week Could Be Huge For AI Stocks
items.summary: AI stocks surged following Intel's strong earnings report, with major tech companies (Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, Apple) set to report earnings this week. These hyperscalers are investing heavily in AI infrastructure and cloud computing, with combined capex of ~$700 billion expected this year. Strong earnings results and signs of AI adoption could drive further gains in AI and semiconductor stocks.

items.title: Where Will Amazon Stock Be in 5 Years?
items.summary: Amazon faces a critical transition period as it invests heavily in AI infrastructure and cloud computing. While the company shows promise in digital advertising (growing 22% YoY) and AI chip design, its massive $200 billion capital expenditure plan raises concerns about near-term profitability and shareholder returns. The stock is rated a cautious buy or hold pending more tangible AI results.

items.title: Booking Holdings: The Glorious Travel Stock You'll Wish You'd Bought Before the Next Boom
items.summary: Booking Holdings stock is down 16% this year amid AI disruption concerns and regulatory probes, but the company reported strong double-digit revenue and EPS growth in 2025. The company is leveraging AI agents, expanding alternative accommodations to 36% of bookings, and growing airline ticket bookings 37%, positioning itself as a dominant travel platform with high-double-digit revenue growth expected in 2026.

items.title: Apple After Cook, And Allbirds After Shoes...
items.summary: Tim Cook is stepping down as Apple CEO after 15 years, raising investor concerns despite the company's strong track record under his leadership. Meanwhile, Allbirds is pivoting away from shoes to become an AI cloud solutions provider called NewBird AI. The article explores how successful companies navigate leadership transitions and business model changes, citing historical examples like Netflix, Berkshire Hathaway, and Samsung that transformed their core businesses.

items.title: S&P 500 Near Highs as Earnings and Fed Outlook Limit Further Gains
items.summary: US stocks are modestly lower on Monday as investors weigh Middle East tensions and prepare for a busy week of Big Tech earnings. Five of the 'Magnificent Seven' companies report this week, with the Federal Reserve meeting also in focus. The S&P 500 has reached record highs around 7,175 but faces consolidation pressure from overbought conditions. Oil prices are rising toward $100/barrel amid supply concerns.

items.title: Amazon Stock Just Keeps Rising. Here's the Latest Catalyst Driving Shares Higher -- and a Close Look at the Stock.
items.summary: Amazon shares have surged over 25% in the last 30 days, driven by major AI infrastructure wins including a deal with Meta to use AWS Graviton5 chips and a $100+ billion commitment from Anthropic over 10 years. Amazon's custom chip business is growing at triple-digit rates with over $20 billion in annual revenue run rate. However, the stock's P/E ratio of 37 and $200 billion capital expenditure expectations for 2026 raise valuation concerns, prompting a 'hold' rating despite strong long-term fundamentals.

items.title: 2 Millionaire-Maker Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks to Buy and Hold
items.summary: The article recommends Micron Technology and Amazon as two AI stocks positioned for long-term growth. Micron produces memory chips essential for AI infrastructure with impressive sequential revenue growth and a 30.6% projected CAGR for the AI industry through 2033. Amazon leverages AI across its businesses including AWS, which achieved its fastest revenue growth in 13 quarters at 24% year-over-year, while the company maintains strong cash reserves of $86.8 billion for future AI investments.

items.title: The Catalyst Is Coming for United Parcel Service. Here's Why Smart Investors Are Buying Before July.
items.summary: United Parcel Service is undergoing a significant turnaround after years of weak financial performance. The company has been investing in technology, reducing staff, and refocusing on high-margin customers while divesting lower-margin business. Management expects an inflection point in the second half of 2026, with revenues resuming growth and margins improving. The stock offers a 6.2% dividend yield, and investors may benefit from buying before second-quarter earnings reveal progress on the turnaround plan.

items.title: 1 Stock to Buy, 1 Stock to Sell This Week: Apple, ExxonMobil
items.summary: With a busy week ahead featuring Fed policy meeting, major tech earnings, and Middle East developments, Apple is positioned for a post-earnings pop driven by strong Services growth and iPhone demand expectations, while ExxonMobil faces headwinds from disappointing guidance and vulnerability to oil price declines amid potential Iran de-escalation.

items.title: Amazon May Be Set to Seize a Once-in-a-Lifetime Opportunity. Here's How Investors Can Benefit.
items.summary: Amazon is positioned to launch a standalone chip business as a new major revenue pillar. The company's in-house designed AI chips (Trainium) and CPUs (Graviton) have reached a $20 billion annual revenue run rate within AWS, with potential to reach $50 billion if sold to third parties. This opportunity could generate significant revenue growth and cost savings for Amazon while establishing chips as a transformative long-term business.

items.title: Nvidia Needs to Watch Out for This Custom Chip Designer (Hint: It's Not Broadcom)
items.summary: Amazon's custom chips, particularly its Trainium training chips, are emerging as a competitive threat to Nvidia's dominance in AI computing. Amazon's chips offer 30% better cost-performance than GPU-based training, with future generations already sold out. While Amazon remains a Nvidia partner, its success in replacing Intel CPUs with Graviton chips suggests it could similarly shift customers away from Nvidia GPUs. AWS's strong profitability and $200 billion capital expenditure commitment position Amazon as a compelling investment opportunity.

items.title: The $720 Billion Capex Trap: 2 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Hyperscalers Spending on Growth While the Rest Spend on Maintenance
items.summary: The top five U.S. hyperscalers plan to spend $720 billion on AI infrastructure in 2026. While Microsoft and Alphabet are positioned to justify their investments through defensible, high-margin applications with existing user bases, Meta, Oracle, and Amazon risk overextension with spending that appears more focused on maintenance and defending existing market share rather than driving new growth.

items.title: This Ultimate AI Stock Has Gained 26% This Year and Still Isn't Done
items.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) has gained 26% year-to-date and is positioned at the center of the AI boom as the primary manufacturer of chips for Nvidia, AMD, and Broadcom. The company demonstrates sustainable growth with 35.1% YoY revenue growth and 58.3% net income growth in Q1, supported by a fortress-like balance sheet with $94.7 billion in cash and a 2.49 current ratio.

items.title: Why I'm Staying on the Sidelines Headed Into Microsoft's Earnings Report
items.summary: Microsoft shares have rallied 14% ahead of its April 29 earnings report, driven by strong Azure cloud growth and impressive contracted revenue backlog of $625 billion.
