However, the author remains cautious due to intense competition from Amazon and Google in cloud computing and AI, significant capital expenditure requirements, and margin pressures from AI infrastructure investments. While Microsoft's valuation appears reasonable at 27x P/E, the author believes execution risks in a transitional AI era make the stock riskier than it appears.

items.title: Is Nvidia Stock Still a Buy After Its Incredible 1,100% Run? Here's the Honest Answer.
items.summary: Nvidia has surged 1,100% since the AI boom began, driven by its dominant GPU chips and CUDA software platform that creates powerful developer lock-in. With fiscal 2026 revenue of $215.9 billion (up 65% YoY) and data center revenue of $62.3 billion in Q4, the company maintains an unmatched competitive position. However, the biggest risk isn't competition but whether AI delivers on its massive investment promises, as current returns on AI infrastructure spending remain minimal despite unprecedented capital deployment.

items.title: X-Energy Stock Opens 31% Above IPO Price As Amazon-Backed Nuclear Play Hits Nasdaq
items.summary: X-Energy (NASDAQ:XE), an Amazon-backed advanced nuclear reactor company, made its public debut with shares opening at $30.11, up 31% from its $23 IPO price. The company raised over $1 billion in an upsized offering, capitalizing on growing investor interest in advanced nuclear energy driven by AI data center power demands and electrification trends. CEO Clay Sell highlighted the company's helium-cooled reactor design and noted a 6-7 year timeline from permit to operation.

items.title: Two Markets In One—Chips And Oil Are Fighting
items.summary: The market is split between AI-driven semiconductor strength and oil price pressures. Intel's strong guidance and AMD's gains are driving an 18-session winning streak in chip stocks, while Brent crude above $104 is creating margin pressure across airlines, logistics, and consumer staples. The Fed decision on April 29, coinciding with mega-cap earnings reports, will determine whether AI infrastructure momentum can overcome energy inflation concerns.

items.title: Nasdaq 100 Marks Fresh Records, Intel Jumps 23% On AI Chip Mania: Stock Market Today
items.summary: The Nasdaq 100 reached fresh record highs on Friday, driven by Intel's blockbuster earnings beat that sparked a broad semiconductor rally. Intel surged 22.6% after exceeding revenue and profit forecasts, benefiting from AI server demand. The S&P 500 rose 0.7% while the Dow slipped 0.1%. Tech stocks led gains, with semiconductor ETFs posting their 18th consecutive day of gains. However, Charter Communications collapsed 23% on a subscriber miss, dragging down cable peers.

items.title: 3 Reasons the Sell-Off in Chewy Is a Gift for Long-Term Investors
items.summary: Chewy's stock has declined nearly 20% year-to-date, presenting an attractive buying opportunity for long-term investors. The company demonstrates strong operating leverage through margin expansion, a recession-resistant business model with 84% recurring autoship sales, and trades at a forward P/E below 17x—a significant discount to competitors like Walmart and Costco despite faster revenue growth and stronger profitability expansion.

items.title: AI Did What Trump's Tariffs Didn't: Revive US Manufacturing
items.summary: U.S. manufacturing is experiencing its hottest pace in nearly four years, driven primarily by AI demand rather than Trump's tariff policies. The S&P Global US Manufacturing PMI jumped to 54.0 in April, with production growth at its fastest pace in four years. AI-driven demand from hyperscalers is fueling capital investment in data centers and infrastructure, benefiting industrial equipment and services companies. Tariffs have instead created input-cost inflation headwinds.

items.title: Why Applied Digital Stock Jumped Today
items.summary: Applied Digital's stock surged 12.09% after securing a 15-year lease deal with an unnamed U.S. hyperscaler for its Delta Forge 1 AI data center facility. The contract, potentially worth up to $7.5 billion, will supply 300 MWs of AI and high-performance computing infrastructure. This marks the company's second major hyperscaler agreement, bringing total contracted lease revenue to over $23 billion.
